summary:
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning:

Tooth eruption involves significant cell proliferation particularly within the external enamel epithelial layer surrounding forming teeth which actively contribute towards their movement outwards against gum tissues; experimental data further confirms that internal biological processes are primary drivers despite supportive structural changes around them. The main focus remains on localized cellular growth facilitating outward progression during emergence into oral cavity spaces designed eventually supporting functional masticatory functions post-eruption completion stages.

agent1 (Round 2 summarized): To summarize Agent1’s reasoning:

Tooth eruption involves significant cell proliferation particularly within the external enamel epithelial layer surrounding forming teeth which actively contribute towards their movement outwards against gum tissues; experimental data further confirms that internal biological processes are primary drivers despite supportive structural changes around them. The main focus remains on localized cellular growth facilitating outward progression during emergence into oral cavity spaces designed eventually supporting functional masticatory functions post-eruption completion stages.
